title: Spring Semester 2025-2026 Course Registration Information
content:
Spring Semester 2025-2026 Course Registration Information

Course Registration Procedures

For the Spring Semester of the 2025–2026 Academic Year, course registration for Graduate, Undergraduate, and Associate Degree students will take place between

February 16–March 1, 2026.

The

add/drop period

. (Course selections can also be made during this period.)

For

first-year students

, course registration will be completed automatically. They do not need to take any action regarding course registration. They are only required to log in to the Student Information System and check that their courses have been assigned.

Procedure

1- Students will select the courses they will take this semester via the course selection screen in the

Student Information System (OBS)

.

2- Students will confirm their course registration online and submit it for their advisor’s online approval.

3- Students who wish to consult their advisor regarding course registration may contact them via messaging through the

STIX

system or by email.

For questions related to course registration procedures, please contact your advisor first.

Student advising procedures will be carried out in accordance with the provisions of our University’s

Student Advising Directive.

Important Notes for Undergraduate and Associate Degree Students

1. Course registration for 1st-year students will be completed automatically. They do not need to take any action regarding course registration. They are only required to log in to the

Student Information System (OBS)

to check that their courses have been assigned.

2. Only 2nd, 3rd, and 4th-year students are required to complete course registration through the

Student Information System (OBS).

3. Students must first register for courses they previously failed or have not yet taken from lower semesters. During registration, the chronological order of lower semesters must be followed. Courses removed from or no longer offered in the curriculum will be replaced by designated alternative courses.

4. The regular semester course load is 30 ECTS, and the maximum course load is 45 ECTS.

5. Students cannot take courses that have not been approved by their academic advisors.

6. With advisor approval, students may retake courses they have previously passed to improve their grade. In such cases, the most recent grade will be valid, regardless of the previous grade. 7. Students will complete course registration via the Student Information System (OBS) and submit it electronically for advisor approval. Once approved by the advisor through the system, the course registration process will be finalized.

8. Students whose course registrations are not approved by their advisors will not be allowed to attend classes.

9. Student status will become active on e-Government (e-Devlet) only after advisor approval of course registration.

10. Students who wish to consult their advisors regarding course registration may contact them via the STIX messaging system or through their email addresses.

11. 1st-year students are not allowed to take courses from upper semesters.

12. Double Major and Minor students may complete their major course registration first and then select their double major or minor program courses through the Student Information System (OBS).

13. Students who have successfully completed all courses from previous semesters and have a GPA of 3.00 or above may, starting from the third semester, take one course from an upper year with advisor approval. Students with a GPA of 3.50 or above may take up to two courses from an upper year.

14. Students who have completed their 2nd year in associate degree programs or their 4th year in bachelor's degree programs and have courses remaining from previous semesters will select their courses through the

Semester ECTS Application and Approval System

and complete their payment transactions. After completing the payment transaction, students will register for their courses themselves through the OBS system.

15. Undergraduate students whose GPA falls below 1.80 will be placed on probationary status. At the end of the 4th semester, students who are still on probation can only take a limited number of courses until they return to good standing:

· With a GPA of 1.60–1.69, a maximum of 2 courses.

· With a GPA of 1.70–1.79, a maximum of 3 courses.

16.

Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences 4th Students

will complete their course registration process without adding the OHS404 Occupational Health and Safety course to their curriculum, which includes the OHS401 Occupational Health and Safety I and OHS402 Occupational Health and Safety II courses, and submit it for advisor approval. The OHS404 Occupational Health and Safety course will be added to the course registration by the advisor. For students who have previously successfully completed either OHS401 or OHS402, the OHS404 course will be added by the advisor as 2 ECTS credits; for those who have previously taken and failed the course or are taking it for the first time, the OHS404 course will be added as 4 ECTS credits. In this context, it is important that our students complete their course registrations leaving the necessary ECTS space for the OHS404 course. The same rule applies to the courses with codes ISG401 and ISG402 in the Molecular Biology and Genetics (Turkish) program. The registration for these courses will be done in a similar manner with the advisor's approval.

Courses Offered for Our International Students

TURK114 Turkish Language II

(Instructor Selçuk Duman –Medium of Instruction: Turkish, content adjusted according to students’ level)

ATA114 Principles of Atatürk and History of Revolutions II

(Instructor Selçuk Duman – Medium of Instruction: English, content adjusted according to students’ level)

International students who wish to take the Turkish Language and Principles of Atatürk and History of Revolutions courses in Turkish via Distance Education may apply to their academic advisor or the respective course instructor to request a course change.

TURK104 Turkish Language II –Instructor Selçuk Duman

ATA104 Principles of Atatürk and History of Revolutions II – Instructor Selçuk Duman

Important Notes for Graduate Students

Students in the scientific preparation stage are required to select the scientific preparation courses listed in the curriculum.

The semester course load for students in the course stage is 30 ECTS.

Students in the thesis stage must register for the Thesis course. (Students in their 5th and 6th semesters may extend their studies by paying an extension fee to the Financial Affairs Office and completing their course registration.)

Students cannot take courses that have not been approved by their academic advisors. ü Course registration must be completed via the Student Information System (OBS) and submitted electronically for advisor approval. Once the advisor approves through the system, the course registration process will be finalized.

Students whose course registrations are not approved by their advisors will not be allowed to attend classes.

Student status will become active on e-Government (e-Devlet) only after advisor approval of course registration.

Students who wish to consult their advisor regarding course registration may contact them via the

STIX

messaging system or through their email addresses.

Important Notes for Medical Faculty Students

1. Course registration for 1st-year students will be completed automatically. They do not need to take any action regarding course registration. They are only required to log in to the Student Information System (OBS) to check that their courses have been assigned.

2. 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, and 6th -year students are required to complete course registration through the Student Information System (OBS).

3. For 1st, 2nd and 3rd year Medical Faculty students, course registration will take place between February 9–22, 2026, and the add/drop period will be between February 23–March 1, 2026. (Course selections may also be made during this period.)

4. For 4th year Medical Faculty students, course registration will take place between February 2–15, 2026, and the add/drop period will be between February 16–22, 2026. (Course selections may also be made during this period.)

5. For 5th -year Medical Faculty students, course registration will take place between January 26–February 8, 2026, and the add/drop period will be between February 9–15, 2026. (Course selections may also be made during this period.)

Important Notes for Dentistry Faculty Students

1. Course registration for 1st-year students will be completed automatically. They do not need to take any action regarding course registration. They are only required to log in to the Student Information System (OBS) to check that their courses have been assigned.

2. 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 5th year students are required to complete course registration through the Student Information System (OBS).

3. For 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 5th year Dentistry Faculty students, course registration will take place between February 16–March 1, 2026. The add/drop period will be between March 2–8, 2026. (Course selections may also be made during this period.)
